skill.name: metacognitive-guard
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to detect reasoning struggle, assess task complexity, and escalate difficult decisions to a deep-thinking partner agent.
skill.summary: Helps assistants recognize complex reasoning tasks and request deeper analysis when needed.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found Markdown guidance and YAML examples only, with no executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Agent Name and YAML Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 14, 53, 58, and 77. These locations contain Markdown inline code and fenced YAML examples that describe use of a Task tool; they are not executable scripts and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown prose and YAML code fences, not Ruby or shell code. No command interpreter, command string construction, or execution API is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3, 10, 35, 57, and 86. These lines are descriptive text or YAML fields about reasoning, descriptions, and failure modes; no cryptographic algorithm or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines contain ordinary skill documentation and example metadata. I found no hashing, encryption, random generation, key handling, or cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Terminology Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md lines 19, 73, and 100. These lines discuss system components, self-assessment, and communication style; they do not inspect host state, environment variables, files, processes, users, or network configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged wording is semantic guidance about analyzing tasks and responses. There is no instruction or code for collecting local system information.

low_findings.title: Minor Operational Concern: Encourages Subagent Escalation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to use a deep-thinking partner agent for complex decisions. This is consistent with the stated purpose, but marketplace users should understand that it can increase agent activity and may expose task context to another configured agent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subagent escalation guidance is explicit and intentional. It is a low security concern because no external service, secret access, or executable behavior is shown in this skill file.

content.value_statement: Complex tasks can produce uncertain or overconfident AI responses. This skill helps assistants identify struggle signals and route difficult decisions to deeper analysis.
actual_capabilities: Defines signs that a task may need deeper reasoning support.
Provides a self-assessment checklist for complex questions.
Shows how to format escalation requests for a deep-thinking partner agent.
Lists response patterns that can indicate reasoning struggle.
Guides assistants to synthesize partner analysis before replying to users.
limitations: Does not include executable code or automated detection logic.
Requires a configured deep-thinking partner agent to use escalation examples directly.
Does not decide task priority or business impact by itself.
May add extra analysis steps for tasks that are actually simple.

use_cases.title: Architecture Decision Support
use_cases.description: Use the checklist before recommending a design when multiple valid approaches have meaningful trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Complex Support Response Review
use_cases.description: Identify uncertain or contradictory answers before sending guidance on high-impact technical issues.

use_cases.title: Agent Workflow Calibration
use_cases.description: Set clear signals for when an assistant should ask a specialized partner agent for deeper analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Check Task Complexity
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before answering a difficult request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the metacognitive guard checklist on this request. Tell me whether it needs deeper analysis and why.

prompt_templates.title: Find Struggle Signals
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a draft assistant response for uncertainty.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft response for hedging, contradictions, deflection, or unclear recommendations. Suggest a clearer response structure.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Escalation Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a complete request for a deep-thinking partner agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an escalation prompt for this decision. Include context, constraints, success criteria, and the specific question.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Partner Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after receiving deeper analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize this partner analysis into a concise user-facing recommendation. Highlight the key insight and next implementation steps.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether Redis or PostgreSQL is better for session storage.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag competing constraints, request deeper analysis if needed, and produce a recommendation that explains trade-offs and migration steps.

output_examples.input: A draft answer says the best choice depends on many factors without making a recommendation.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify hedging and deflection, then suggest a clearer answer with confidence level, assumptions, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: A partner agent returns a long architecture analysis.
output_examples.output: The skill would guide the assistant to summarize the key insight, state the recommendation first, and offer an implementation plan.

best_practices: Use the checklist before answering decisions with high rework cost.
Include context, constraints, and success criteria in every escalation request.
Synthesize partner analysis into a direct recommendation instead of forwarding it unchanged.
anti_patterns: Escalating simple questions that do not need deeper analysis.
Using the skill to avoid making a clear recommendation.
Pasting partner output directly without adapting it for the user.

faq.question: Does this skill run code or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance and does not include executable code.

faq.question: What problem does it solve?
faq.answer: It helps assistants recognize when a task is too complex for a quick answer and needs deeper reasoning.

faq.question: Does it require another agent?
faq.answer: Direct escalation examples require a configured deep-thinking partner agent. The checklist can still be used without one.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It improves reasoning workflow, but high-impact decisions still need appropriate human review.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows that support structured reasoning or agent escalation.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid using it for routine tasks where the answer is clear and escalation would slow the workflow.
